title: Iraq vs Norway Picks, Predictions & Betting Odds: World Cup 2026 Preview - Yahoo Sports
original_text:
Iraq vs Norway Picks, Predictions & Betting Odds: World Cup 2026 Preview
Iraq face Norway in World Cup 2026 Group I action at Gillette Stadium, Boston (Foxborough) on June 16, kicking off at 6:00 PM ET. Norway are heavy favorites at -460 to -650 across books, with Iraq available at +1300 to +1500 and the draw priced at +600 to +650.
Date: Tuesday, June 16, 2026
Kickoff: 6:00 PM ET
Venue: Gillette Stadium, Boston (Foxborough), USA
Stage: World Cup 2026 Group I, Matchday 6
TV/Streaming: Fox, Telemundo (USA)
Why This Game Matters
Group I places Iraq and Norway alongside France and Senegal, two sides that will contest most of the qualifying real estate at the top of the table. That makes this opening fixture between the group’s most contrasting sides a potentially decisive one: a Norway win virtually guarantees them a competitive platform heading into fixtures against France and Senegal, while Iraq, returning to the World Cup for the first time since 1986, cannot afford to fall three points behind on Matchday 1 if they hold any realistic hopes of advancing.
Advertisement
Best World Cup 2026 Offer
Betfred 4.5 Welcome Bonus Bet and Get New Bettor Bonus of £50 in Free Bets BET NOW
Our Pick
Norway to win this match is the headline selection, with the best available price at -460 reflecting their dominant qualifying campaign and the class gap between the two squads. At that price the value is thin on the moneyline, which makes Norway -1.5 on the Asian handicap or the Over 3 goals the sharper angles given Norway’s 37 goals in eight qualifying matches.
Norway arrive as one of European qualifying’s most convincing performers: eight wins from eight, 37 goals scored, five conceded, including a 4-1 away victory over Italy that announced them as genuine contenders. Coach Ståle Solbakken has built a side that blends the explosive finishing of Erling Haaland with the creative intelligence of Martin Ødegaard, and a supporting cast capable of punishing transitional errors in volume. The Norway betting odds of -460 to -650 across the three major books reflect a wide market consensus that this result is close to a formality.
Advertisement
Iraq represent the counter-narrative. Coach Graham Arnold, the first Australian to take two different nations to a World Cup, has transformed a side that was in crisis when he took over in 2025 into one that qualified through sheer collective discipline and resilience. Their path here included a 40-hour journey to Monterrey amid Middle East airspace closures, a come-from-behind win over Bolivia, and a last-minute VAR penalty scored by Amir Al-Ammari to see off the UAE. Arnold’s own framing tells you everything about how Iraq intend to approach this tournament: “All the pressure is on France to win it, the pressure’s on Norway and Senegal to get through, the pressure is not on Iraq.”
The Iraq vs Norway prediction hinges on whether Iraq’s defensive solidity, which was good enough to qualify through Asia and the inter-confederation play-offs, can withstand the relentless movement of Haaland and the incisiveness of Ødegaard in the final third. Iraq are a compact, counter-attacking side that conceded only nine goals across nine qualifying matches. Norway will have the ball and will probe. The question is whether Iraq can stay organised long enough to make it uncomfortable, or whether Norway’s firepower ultimately tells.
Recent Form & Trends
Iraq last five matches:
Venezuela (N): Lost 0-2 (Friendly, June 9, 2026)
Spain (A): Drew 1-1 (Friendly, June 4, 2026)
Andorra (N): Won 1-0 (Friendly, May 29, 2026)
Bolivia (N): Won 2-1 (World Cup Qualifying, March 31, 2026)
Jordan (N): Lost 0-1 (Arab Cup, December 12, 2025)
Advertisement
Iraq’s pre-tournament friendlies tell a nuanced story. The 1-1 draw with Spain suggests they are not simply a team that will be overrun by better opposition, but the 2-0 loss to Venezuela days later is a reminder that their attacking output is modest and their results are streaky. Arnold’s side are built to grind, not to dominate, and their scoring numbers across all competitions reflect a team that relies on structure over individual brilliance for large portions of games.
Norway last five matches:
Morocco (N): Drew 1-1 (Friendly, June 7, 2026)
Sweden (H): Won 3-1 (Friendly, June 1, 2026)
Switzerland (H): Drew 0-0 (Friendly, March 31, 2026)
Netherlands (A): Lost 1-2 (Friendly, March 27, 2026)
Italy (A): Won 4-1 (World Cup Qualifying, November 16, 2025)
Norway’s friendly results show a side willing to manage load and context ahead of the tournament: the 0-0 against Switzerland and the defeat to the Netherlands suggest Solbakken has been experimenting rather than chasing results. The 3-1 win over Sweden just before the squad assembled is a more reliable indicator of their attacking capacity, and the 4-1 demolition of Italy in the final qualifier remains the most compelling single data point. Norway arrive with momentum, depth and a clear identity.
Injuries, Suspensions & Roster News
No confirmed injuries or suspensions have been reported for either squad ahead of this fixture. Both squads have been officially announced for the tournament, and preparations appear to have proceeded without significant disruption on the pitch.
Advertisement
For Iraq, the most notable pre-tournament incident was off the field. Aymen Hussein, the talisman who scored the decisive qualifying goal against Bolivia and is among Iraq’s most important attacking players, was reportedly detained for questioning at Chicago Airport on arrival in the United States. The hold lasted several hours and raised questions about the disruption to Iraq’s squad preparations, though he has since linked up with the group. Arnold has built a tight squad culture and the expectation is that this will not affect team cohesion heading into Matchday 1.
For Norway, the squad is at full strength. Erling Haaland and Martin Ødegaard are both available and expected to start. The depth in attacking positions, with Alexander Sørloth, Antonio Nusa and Jørgen Strand Larsen all fit, gives Solbakken genuine options to rotate without weakening the side significantly. Veteran goalkeeper Ørjan Nyland is expected to start in goal.
Expected Lineups
Iraq (4-4-2): Jalal Hassan; Frans Putros, Rebin Sulaka, Merchas Doski, Hussein Ali; Ali Jasim, Amir Al-Ammari (c), Ibrahim Bayesh, Youssef Amyn; Aymen Hussein, Ali Al-Hamadi
Advertisement
Norway (4-3-3): Ørjan Nyland; Julian Ryerson, Kristoffer Ajer, Leo Østigård, Marcus Holmgren Pedersen; Sander Berge, Patrick Berg, Martin Ødegaard (c); Antonio Nusa, Erling Haaland, Alexander Sørloth
Predicted XI: squads to be confirmed.
Key Matchup to Watch
The contest between Iraq’s midfield block and Norway’s central creative axis is where this game is decided. Amir Al-Ammari operates as Iraq’s midfield anchor, tasked with limiting the space through which Martin Ødegaard can operate between the lines. Ødegaard’s ability to receive in tight areas, turn and pick a pass into Haaland’s movement is the mechanism through which Norway generate their most dangerous transitions. Iraq’s defensive record across qualifying, four clean sheets in nine matches, suggests they can organise a shape capable of limiting space centrally. Whether Al-Ammari can stay disciplined enough to deny Ødegaard the half-turns that unlock defenses, while also tracking runs from arriving midfielders, will define how long Iraq stay competitive in this match.

Best Bets & Expert Picks

Norway to Win (-460, BetOnline) is the anchor pick, though at that price it functions as a foundation rather than a value play on its own. Norway won all eight qualifiers and scored 37 goals in the process. Iraq have not appeared at a World Cup since 1986 and face the most formidable opener imaginable. The result direction is not in serious doubt.
Advertisement
Over 3 Goals (-102 at BetOnline on the Over 3.0 line) is the sharpest angle here. Norway averaged well over four goals per qualifying match, and Iraq’s attacking limitations make this a likely one-sided affair in terms of possession and shots. The 3.0 total is generous given Norway’s output, and the best available price on the over at -102 via BetOnline makes this the most attractive play on the board for the Iraq vs Norway picks.
Erling Haaland Anytime Scorer is the scorer market selection. With 55 international goals in 50 caps, Haaland is Norway’s primary and most consistent attacking outlet. Iraq’s centre-backs have limited experience against a striker of his quality, and Haaland’s movement into the channels and across the front post make him a near-constant threat in any match where Norway dominate possession. Available across all three approved books.
Norway -1.5 (Asian Handicap) is the optional fourth pick for bettors who want Norway exposure with improved value over the moneyline. Given the scale of Norway’s qualifying dominance and Iraq’s modest offensive output, a multi-goal Norway win is a realistic base-case scenario, and the handicap line offers a better return than backing a Norway win at -460 to -650.
Betting Odds & Lines
Here are the current Iraq vs Norway betting odds across the three approved books for this World Cup 2026 Group I fixture.
Advertisement
The best available price on Norway is -460 across the full market. For the goals market, BetOnline offers Over 3 at -102 and Under 3 at -118, making the over the sharper side of the line.
How to Watch & Where to Bet
How to Watch
Iraq vs Norway will be broadcast in the United States on Fox and Telemundo. The match kicks off at 6:00 PM ET on June 16, 2026, from Gillette Stadium in Boston (Foxborough). International viewers can find coverage on TyC Sports and TV Publica (Argentina), Globo and SporTV (Brazil), CTV, TSN and RDS (Canada), ITV and BBC (UK), TF1 and beIN Sports (France), ARD, ZDF and MagentaTV (Germany), SBS and Optus Sport (Australia), and NOS (Netherlands).
Advertisement
How to Bet
To get the best Iraq vs Norway odds and place your bets on this World Cup 2026 Group I match, follow these steps:
Choose one of the approved sportsbooks: BetOnline, Lucky Rebel, or BetNow.
Create an account and complete any required identity verification.
Make a deposit using your preferred payment method. BetNow supports crypto payments.
Navigate to Soccer or Football, then World Cup 2026 and Group I.
Locate the Iraq vs Norway fixture scheduled for June 16.
Select your markets: match result, goals over/under, or player scorer markets.
Compare the prices across all three books before placing. The best available Norway moneyline is -460; the best Iraq price is +1500 at BetOnline and Lucky Rebel.
Confirm your bet slip and stake responsibly.
Responsible Gambling
Gambling should be approached as entertainment, not as a source of income. If betting is affecting your finances, relationships or wellbeing, help is available 24/7 by calling the National Problem Gambling Helpline at 1-800-GAMBLER (1-800-426-2537), or by visiting ncpgambling.org or Gamblers Anonymous. Set deposit limits before you bet and never chase losses.
